# Team Flow — shared rules
_Generated by `/teams:init`. Edit freely below the imported block; re-running `/teams:init` preserves your edits._

# imported from CLAUDE.md (auto-regenerated by /teams:init — edit CLAUDE.md, not this block)
- [must-fix] Forbidden zones: none path-based. Invariants apply instead — see `.flow-spec/project.md` §Hard rules and `CLAUDE.md` §Conventions & gotchas. Most load-bearing: audio is triggered from `DiceNotifier` only (the lone exception is the preview button on `chord_reference_screen.dart`); the consolidated `_ChordMeta` record map in `lib/models/chord_type.dart` must not be split back into parallel tables; `test/chord_test.dart` (60+ music-theory cases) is never edited to make a failure go away — fix the code instead.
- [must-fix] Dependency policy: no new deps without explicit user approval. `music_notes` is the canonical interval/pitch math library — do not reintroduce raw `(octave + 1) * 12 + semitone` formulas or modular `% 12` interval math. `equatable`, `google_fonts`, and `flame` are deliberately absent and must not be re-added. `flutter_midi_pro` (^3.1.6) is the audio backend; `sensors_plus` is the accelerometer source; `path_provider` + `share_plus` is the MIDI export path. (CLAUDE.md §Conventions & gotchas)
- [must-fix] Naming conventions: top-level constants use `kCamelCase` (e.g. `kRollAnimationDuration`); private members use `_underscorePrefix`; enum values are `lowerCamelCase`; long-file sections are demarcated with `// ─── Section Name ───`. Note display is **sharp-canonical** — `Chord.name`, `Chord.chordNotesWithOctave`, `noteToMidi`, and `chord_test.dart` are sharp-only; flats appear only at render time via `formatNote` / `ChordDisplayX` in `lib/utils/note_format.dart`. (CLAUDE.md §Conventions & gotchas)
- [must-fix] Error-handling pattern: after every `await` in a notifier/state-mutating method, re-check `ref.mounted` (in Notifier methods) or `mounted` (in `State` subclasses). Use `unawaited(...)` explicitly for fire-and-forget calls — never drop futures silently. Models implement `==` / `hashCode` manually with `Object.hash(...)` (no `equatable`). `copyWith` uses a `clearX: bool` flag when a field can be intentionally nulled. (CLAUDE.md §Conventions & gotchas)
# end imported

## Team-wide
- [must-fix] No new dependencies without an explicit plan step or user approval.
- [must-fix] No silencing of lint or type-check rules to make exit gates pass.
- [must-fix] All design choices must cite the brief, the plan, or `CLAUDE.md` when challenged.
- [should-fix] Prefer Serena symbol tools (`find_symbol`, `replace_symbol_body`, …) over `Read`/`Grep`/`Edit` when changing whole symbols.

## Project context
- Language: Dart 3 / Flutter (iOS + Android, portrait-locked, fully offline)
- Test roots: test/
- Public-API entry points: n/a (mobile app, not a library; `lib/main.dart` is the runtime entry)

## Senior-specialist baseline (applies to every agent — calibrate to a strong senior engineer, not a junior contributor)
These rules are the team's canonical bar. Per-role files reinforce them with role-specific tactics; they do **not** restate them.

- [must-fix] Root cause > symptom. Never paper over a bug with try/catch, fallback, default, or special-case. If you can't explain *why* the bug existed, the fix is incomplete — keep digging or push back.
- [must-fix] YAGNI. No abstractions, generics, interfaces, strategy patterns, dependency-injection seams, or config knobs without a second concrete caller **in the same change**. Three duplicated lines beat a premature abstraction.
- [must-fix] Smallest possible change. The diff matches the requested task — no "while I was here" refactors, formatting churn in untouched files, or unrelated dep bumps. Surface tangential cleanups in `phase-N-result.md` under "Out-of-scope follow-ups", not in the diff.
- [must-fix] No half-finished implementations. Every reachable code path is fully implemented, or explicitly rejected with a typed error / `UnimplementedError` / `panic!` / `throw` plus a one-line note. No silent no-ops, no empty `catch` blocks, no `return null` "for now".
- [must-fix] No backwards-compatibility shims for code with no callers. When you remove a symbol, also remove its forwarders, deprecated aliases, type re-exports, and "removed in vX" comments — in the same change.
- [must-fix] Trust the type system. Do not add runtime checks for invariants the compiler/analyzer already enforces (null guards on non-nullable params, type checks on already-narrowed values, length guards on fixed-size arrays). Validate **only** at system boundaries (user input, network, disk, FFI, deserialization).
- [must-fix] Comments default to **none**. Justify a comment only when the *why* is non-obvious — a hidden constraint, a workaround for a specific bug, behavior that would surprise a future reader. Never explain *what* the code does (well-named identifiers do that). Never reference the current task / PR / brief / phase ("added for X", "used by Y", "TODO from issue #123") — these rot the moment the change ships.
- [must-fix] No leftover scaffolding: no `print` / `console.log` / `debugPrint` / `println!` / `fmt.Println`, no commented-out code, no unused imports, no `TODO` / `FIXME` / `XXX` without an explicit plan citation.
- [should-fix] Match existing patterns. Consistency with the rest of the codebase outranks personal preference; novelty needs a written justification.
- [should-fix] Read before write. Before editing a symbol, read its current definition and at least one call site (`find_referencing_symbols`). Do not edit by inference from grep results alone.
